text.title: django-quality-security Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: HSTS ramp + CSP migration for a freshly-launched Django 5.2 service over 3 weeks.

example.code:
Week 0 (initial public launch): SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 300 SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = False # subdomain readiness unknown SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD = False CSP middleware in report-only mode: default-src 'self' script-src 'self' 'nonce-{request.csp_nonce}' img-src 'self' data: https: Cache layer: no-store on HTML responses to keep nonces unique
Week 1 (after 24h clean staging telemetry): SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 3600 Move CSP to enforce mode after fixing 4 inline-style violations Add CSP report-uri pointing to Sentry to surface ongoing violations
Week 2 (no new browser CSP errors in Sentry): SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000 SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = True Continue monitoring
Week 4 (HSTS at production value for 2+ weeks): SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD = True Submit domain to hstspreload.org
example.note: Ramp avoids the worst HSTS antipattern: cannot reach the site if HTTPS breaks. Each step exits only after telemetry is clean.

example.code: # .github/workflows/security.yml (excerpt) - name: Django check --deploy run: | uv run python manage.py check --deploy \ --fail-level WARNING \ --settings=config.settings.production
example.note: Single command blocks merge on any WARNING / ERROR — the cheapest security gate that exists for Django.
